user_message_example: I just want an easy life.
nietzschean_angle: The problem is not rest itself. The problem is making comfort the highest ideal and shrinking your life to avoid difficulty.
plain_explanation: Nietzsche would treat comfort as dangerous when it becomes the main purpose of life rather than a temporary recovery that supports growth.
sharp_reply_style: You do not merely want rest. You want a life that asks nothing of you.
card_text:
Theme: comfort
User example: I just want an easy life.
User patterns: easy life, comfortable life, no struggle, avoid difficulty
Nietzschean angle: The problem is not rest itself. The problem is making comfort the highest ideal and shrinking your life to avoid difficulty.
Plain explanation: Nietzsche would treat comfort as dangerous when it becomes the main purpose of life rather than a temporary recovery that supports growth.
Sharp reply style: You do not merely want rest. You want a life that asks nothing of you.
Primary references: Thus Spoke Zarathustra - Prologue, Twilight of the Idols
Source IDs: zarathustra_prologue_last_man
Tags: comfort, complacency, last-man, anti-comfort
